claim: Retatrutide is a single peptide agonist at GIP, GLP-1 and glucagon receptors.
public_wording: Retatrutide is being studied as a triple hormone-receptor agonist.
limitations: Mechanistic activity does not itself establish clinical benefit or approval.

claim: Structural studies describe retatrutide engagement of GLP-1R, GIPR and GCGR.
public_wording: Laboratory structural studies support activity at all three target receptors.
limitations: Structural evidence is not clinical-outcome evidence.

claim: In the phase 2 obesity trial, the 12 mg group had approximately 24.2% mean weight reduction at 48 weeks.
public_wording: A phase 2 trial reported substantial average weight reduction at the highest studied dose.
limitations: Trial estimate, not a guarantee; retatrutide remains investigational.

claim: TRIUMPH-1 sponsor topline results reported 28.3% mean weight reduction at 80 weeks at 12 mg.
public_wording: Sponsor-reported phase 3 topline results were positive; full peer-reviewed publication and regulator review remain outstanding.
limitations: Topline sponsor disclosure, not yet a full peer-reviewed trial report in this baseline.

claim: Early phase evidence supported dose escalation into later type 2 diabetes trials.
public_wording: Early clinical testing supported continued development in type 2 diabetes.

claim: Phase 2 retatrutide improved HbA1c and bodyweight compared with controls.
public_wording: A phase 2 trial reported improved glucose control and bodyweight outcomes.

claim: TRANSCEND-T2D-1 phase 3 results support glycaemic and weight efficacy in adults with type 2 diabetes.
public_wording: A published phase 3 trial reported improvements in glucose control and bodyweight.
limitations: Dedicated long-term outcome trials remain relevant.

claim: A phase 2a substudy reported substantial liver-fat reductions at higher doses.
public_wording: A substudy reported reductions in liver fat.
limitations: Imaging endpoint; clinical liver-outcome benefit is not established.

claim: A DXA substudy found higher-dose retatrutide reduced total fat mass and did not show a disproportionate lean-mass loss relative to other obesity treatments.
public_wording: A body-composition substudy reported substantial fat-mass reduction.
limitations: Substudy attrition and secondary endpoint design.

claim: Gastrointestinal adverse events are the most consistently reported tolerability issue across clinical trials.
public_wording: Gastrointestinal side effects have been common in trials.
limitations: Frequency and severity depend on dose, escalation and study population.

claim: Dose-dependent heart-rate increases were observed in the phase 2 obesity trial.
public_wording: Heart-rate increases were observed in a phase 2 trial and require continued safety evaluation.
limitations: Long-term clinical significance remains under investigation.

claim: Products sold as retatrutide outside authorised trials cannot be assumed to be authentic, pure or correctly dosed.
public_wording: Do not use products claiming to be retatrutide outside authorised clinical trials.
limitations: Official sponsor warning; jurisdiction-specific enforcement and regulator notices require surveillance.

claim: Retatrutide is investigational and not approved for public use as of the baseline date.
public_wording: Retatrutide remains investigational and is not approved for public use.
limitations: Time-sensitive status; regulator databases must be checked at every public-release review.

claim: The development programme progressed from translational discovery into phase 2 and phase 3 trials.
public_wording: Retatrutide has progressed through a multi-stage clinical development programme.
limitations: Development progress is not regulatory approval.

claim: The TRIUMPH programme evaluates obesity and selected obesity-related conditions under registrational designs.
public_wording: The phase 3 programme includes obesity and related conditions.
limitations: Protocol design does not establish final benefit.

claim: TRIUMPH-3 evaluates retatrutide in a cardiovascular-disease population over an extended follow-up period.
public_wording: A phase 3 trial is evaluating retatrutide in people with cardiovascular disease.
limitations: Registry protocol; final results are not represented by this claim.
